qas.q: Characteristics of an effective team
qas.a: involvement, ideas are encouraged, trusting, high problem solving skills

qas.q: General duties of a nurse aide
qas.a: basic nursing skills, basic personal care skills, interpersonal skills

qas.q: to stop infection how many links must be broken
qas.a: at least one link must be broken

qas.q: list the items in PPE
qas.a: gloves, gown, mask, goggles

qas.q: anaerobic organisms thrive in what environment
qas.a: an environment with no oxygen

qas.q: characteristics that put geriatric clients at risk for falling with potential injury
qas.a: unsafe conditions, spills, bed rails not on properly, wheelchairs or beds not in a locked positon

qas.q: list the barriers to successful communication
qas.a: noisy room, no privacy, talking too fast, sender or reciever is mentally confused, blindness, speech difficulty, prejudices, frustration, attitudes, cultural differences

qas.q: alcohol based hand rubs may be used when
qas.a: you dont have access to water, before/after eating, handling food, or resident care

qas.q: PASS stands for
qas.a: Pull, aim, squeeze, sweep

qas.q: define vulnerable adults
qas.a: older adults, adults with sores, diabetes, or bad health

qas.q: define culture
qas.a: a view of the world as well as a set of values, beliefs, and traditions, that are handed down from generation to generation

qas.q: who supervises nurse aides
qas.a: nurses, charge nurses

qas.q: what is an HAI (healthcare associated infection)
qas.a: an infection that a resident gets while staying or living in a healthcare setting

qas.q: standard precautions are
qas.a: the basic tasks that health care workers must do when caring for each and every resident in order to prevent and control spread of infection

qas.q: most important step to prevent the spread of disease
qas.a: handwashing

qas.q: list the components of effective communication
qas.a: message to be delivered, sender of the message, reciever of the message, feedback about the message

qas.q: nursing assiatants are responsible for
qas.a: performing delegated basic nursing skills, delegated personal care skills, and apropriate interpsersonal skills

qas.q: list examples of when you should wear gloves while performing care
qas.a: when you may come into contact with blood or body fluids, sores or cuts, mucus membranes, during mouth care, pericare, shaving etc

qas.q: examples of a localized infection
qas.a: an infection found in one part of the body and symptoms are limited to that one part of the body, Ex. an infected finger

qas.q: steps to prevent falls include
qas.a: assist with walking, provide assistance/supervision, keep environment free of clutter, keep walkways clear, observe frequently

qas.q: steps to communicate with clients from another culture
qas.a: cultural sensitivity, accept each resident as an individual, follow care plan, respect, greet respectfully, follow appropriate cultural preference

qas.q: considerations caring for a person with impaired vison
qas.a: turn up lighting for better eyesight and assist when needed

qas.q: purpose of OBRA
qas.a: to require that long term care facilities follow safety policies and procedures

qas.q: symptoms of a UTI include
qas.a: fever and chills, pain when using the bathroom, bad or strong smelling urine with possible blood in it, resident states it hurts to pee or their pee stinks

qas.q: which clients do we use standard precautions with
qas.a: all clients

qas.q: medical asepsis is
qas.a: also called clean technique, practices used to remove or destroy microorganisms and to prevent their spread

qas.q: steps in helping a conscious choking client are
qas.a: get resident to cough, ask someone to get a nurse, stay with person, follow facilities procedures for clearing an obstructed airway

qas.q: steps to enhance communication with a client are
qas.a: keep messages simple, repeat often, avoid medical terms, pay attention to nonverbal behavior, use language dictionary if needed and available

qas.q: examples of questions to enhance therapeutic communication
qas.a: where would you like to begin?, what does the voice seem to be saying?, so this causes you to feel angry?

qas.q: medications a nursing assistant can administer
qas.a: none!!!

qas.q: purpose of infection prevention
qas.a: to keep infections from spreading from resident to resident or keep infections from happening

qas.q: aerobic microorganisms thrive in
qas.a: an environment with oxygen

qas.q: when providing personal care to a resident the nursing assistant should encourage the client to
qas.a: be independent but ask for help if they need it
